files.purpose: Static About page for the Saltgrass Modular client site. Renders founder story, differentiators, fabrication facility info, team bios, certifications, and a CTA banner — all hardcoded copy, no dynamic data fetching.

exposesEndpoints.detail: Astro static page — rendered at build time, no auth guard

findings.note: All stats (3+ years, 50+ projects, 99% satisfaction) are hardcoded copy. If this data changes, it requires a code edit rather than a CMS update — drift risk over time.
Team section uses placeholder gradient divs instead of actual photos for Engineering Team and Installation Specialists — placeholder state shipped to production.

Images (/img/about-hero.jpg, /img/dylan-portrait.jpg, /img/facility-tour.jpg) are referenced but their existence in /public is not verifiable from this file alone — broken image risk if assets are not deployed.

discoveredConventions.name: Astro component composition pattern
discoveredConventions.evidence: Uses BaseLayout + Hero + CtaBanner as composable section components — consistent pattern likely repo-wide for Saltgrass Modular pages

files.purpose: Astro API route that acts as a health-check aggregator for the Saltgrass Modular client site. Probes the LPAI NestJS backend's /api/health endpoint and surfaces a combined frontend+backend+database status JSON response for synthetic monitoring.

connectionsOut.detail: 5-second AbortSignal timeout; proxies backend health + extracts database check status

exposesEndpoints.detail: No auth guard — public endpoint. Returns HealthStatus JSON with frontend/backend/database checks, version, uptime, and overall status.

findings.note: uptime field is set to startTime (Date.now() at request start) rather than process uptime or a stable timestamp — this will always reflect request-start epoch ms, not actual service uptime.
statusCode logic sets 503 for both 'timeout' and 'error' states — the comment and overallStatus distinguish them but HTTP callers only see 503, losing the timeout signal.
PUBLIC_LOCATION_ID is forwarded to the backend health check to scope the DB probe — correct multi-tenant pattern for a single-location client site.
No auth on this endpoint — intentional for uptime probing, but the response includes database status which could inform an attacker whether the DB is reachable. Low practical risk but worth noting.

discoveredConventions.name: PUBLIC_API_URL + PUBLIC_LOCATION_ID env pattern
discoveredConventions.evidence: Both vars referenced at top of file as Astro env imports with Railway fallback URL — consistent pattern across all saltgrass-modular API routes

discoveredConventions.name: Silent-fail / empty-response on error
discoveredConventions.evidence: AbortError caught and returns degraded status rather than throwing — same silent-fail convention seen in s.ts and t.ts

files.purpose: Astro API route that proxies and rewrites the LPAI analytics tracking script. Fetches the JS bundle from the backend, rewrites the tracking URL to point to the same-origin /api/t path (ad-blocker bypass), and serves it with a 1-hour cache.

connectionsOut.detail: Fetches the analytics JS bundle as text; rewrites beacon URL before serving

exposesEndpoints.detail: Public, no auth. Returns JavaScript (Content-Type: application/javascript) with 1-hour cache. Returns 204 empty body on backend error.

findings.note: Regex rewrite `var u="[^"]+"` assumes the backend tracking script uses a specific variable name/format. If the backend script changes that variable name, the rewrite silently fails and the beacon URL is not patched — tracking breaks without any error signal.

On backend error, returns HTTP 204 with empty body and Content-Type: application/javascript. Browsers will execute the empty response silently — no JS error but tracking is fully dead. Silent failure is intentional per comment.
Script is cached 1 hour (max-age=3600). If the backend tracking script is updated, cached clients will not pick it up for up to an hour.

discoveredConventions.name: Same-origin proxy for ad-blocker bypass
discoveredConventions.evidence: Rewrites tracking URL from absolute backend URL to /api/t — repo-wide pattern for LPAI client sites to bypass ad-blocker domain blacklists

files.purpose: Astro API route that proxies analytics tracking beacon POSTs to the LPAI backend. Receives a raw body from the client-side tracking script and forwards it to the backend /api/website-clients/track endpoint, acting as a same-origin relay to bypass ad blockers.

connectionsOut.detail: Raw body passthrough; response body also streamed back directly from backend

exposesEndpoints.detail: Public, no auth. Returns backend response body + status verbatim. Returns 204 empty on error.

findings.note: No validation or rate-limiting on the proxy — any client can POST arbitrary payloads to the backend tracking endpoint via this relay. The backend must enforce its own validation and rate limits.

Content-Type is hardcoded as 'application/json' when forwarding to backend, but request.text() reads the raw body regardless of incoming Content-Type. If the tracking script sends non-JSON, the backend may reject it.
res.body is streamed directly as the response. If the backend returns a streaming or chunked response, edge-runtime stream piping behavior in Astro SSR may vary.

discoveredConventions.name: Same-origin proxy for ad-blocker bypass
discoveredConventions.evidence: /api/t is the beacon relay paired with /api/s (script loader) — both implement the same-origin proxy pattern for LPAI analytics on client sites

files.purpose: Dynamic Astro blog post detail page for Saltgrass Modular. Uses SSG (getStaticPaths) to pre-render all published posts. Displays article content, author bio, related posts, a per-post CTA block with gradient support, SEO/OG meta, BlogPosting JSON-LD schema, a reading-progress bar, and a back-to-top button.

connectionsOut.detail: Imports getBlogPosts, getBlogPostBySlug, getRelatedPosts, getCtaForPost, getBlogChrome, getAuthorName, getAuthorAvatar, estimateReadTime — these in turn call LPAI backend API endpoints
Called via getBlogPosts() in getStaticPaths() to enumerate all slugs at build time
Called via getBlogPostBySlug() — fetches full post content including SEO fields
Called via getRelatedPosts() — fetches up to 4 same-category posts, excludes current slug, limits to 3
Called via getCtaForPost() — fetches all CTAs and selects one by ctaType slug or hash-based fallback
Called via getBlogChrome() — fetches custom header/footer HTML and CSS injections

exposesEndpoints.detail: Static pre-rendered at build time via getStaticPaths. Redirects to /blog if slug not found.

findings.note: chrome.headerHtml, chrome.footerHtml, and post.content are all rendered with set:html (raw HTML injection). If the backend returns malicious HTML (e.g. from a compromised CMS record), it executes in the browser. Trust is entirely in the backend/admin data.

CTA gradient detection: `ctaIsTailwindGradient` checks if gradient starts with 'from-' to distinguish Tailwind class from inline CSS. If a Tailwind gradient class doesn't start with 'from-' (e.g. 'bg-gradient-to-r from-...'), the detection fails and ctaInlineBg is set incorrectly.
getStaticPaths calls getBlogPosts() which uses limit=100. Sites with >100 posts will silently drop slugs from the pre-rendered set — those posts will 404 at runtime.
All API calls correctly scope to PUBLIC_LOCATION_ID — correct single-tenant isolation for this client site.

On each page build, 5 separate API calls are made (posts list, post by slug, related posts, CTAs, chrome settings). Promise.all parallelizes 3 of them but the initial getBlogPosts() in getStaticPaths runs sequentially before the detail rendering phase.

discoveredConventions.name: BlogPosting JSON-LD schema injection
discoveredConventions.evidence: Inline <script type='application/ld+json'> with BlogPosting schema including headline, author, publisher, datePublished, wordCount — likely a repo-wide pattern for blog detail pages

discoveredConventions.name: astro:page-load event listener pattern
discoveredConventions.evidence: Client-side scripts initialize on both DOMContentLoaded and astro:page-load to handle Astro view transitions

files.purpose: Blog listing index page for Saltgrass Modular. Fetches all published posts and category list at build time, renders a featured post hero, a filterable grid of remaining posts, and client-side category pill + search input filtering (no server round-trips for filtering).

connectionsOut.detail: Imports getBlogPosts, getBlogCategories, getAuthorName, estimateReadTime — these call the LPAI backend API
Called via getBlogPosts() — fetches all published posts for the listing page
Called via getBlogCategories() — fetches taxonomy categories; falls back to deriving categories client-side from post data if empty

exposesEndpoints.detail: Static Astro page — rendered at build time. No auth guard.

findings.note: getBlogPosts() uses limit=100. If the location has >100 posts, the listing silently truncates — no pagination UI is rendered. The filter/search only operates on the pre-fetched 100-post window.

Featured post selection: `posts.find(p => p.heroStyle === 'featured') || posts[0]`. If no post has heroStyle='featured', the newest post (posts[0]) is featured by API return order — this is implicit and may surprise content editors.

Client-side category and search filtering operates on data-attributes injected at build time (data-cat, data-title, data-excerpt). All filtering is zero-latency JS with no network round-trips — good pattern.

Category fallback logic derives categories from post data if API returns empty array. The fallback uses post.category values directly as both slug and name, so category slugs will not be URL-safe if post.category contains spaces or special chars.
Both API calls correctly scope to PUBLIC_LOCATION_ID — correct single-tenant isolation for this client site.

discoveredConventions.name: astro:page-load event listener pattern
discoveredConventions.evidence: initBlogFilters() bound to both DOMContentLoaded and astro:page-load for view-transition compatibility — matches pattern in [slug].astro

discoveredConventions.name: Build-time data-attribute injection for client-side filtering
discoveredConventions.evidence: data-cat / data-title / data-excerpt injected on each post card element, enabling zero-latency JS filtering without any client-side API calls

files.purpose: Astro API route that generates a standard RSS 2.0 XML feed for the Saltgrass Modular blog, fetching the 30 most recent published posts from the LPAI backend via the shared getBlogPosts() lib function and returning them as an application/xml response.

connectionsOut.detail: getBlogPosts() internally calls /api/blog/posts?locationId=...&status=published&limit=100 on the LPAI Railway backend
Called transitively through getBlogPosts() in lib/blog.ts; only top 30 posts are rendered despite limit=100 fetch

exposesEndpoints.detail: Returns Content-Type: application/xml. No auth guard — publicly accessible RSS feed.

findings.note: getBlogPosts() fetches up to 100 posts from the backend (limit=100) but rss.xml.ts only renders the first 30 (.slice(0, 30)). If there are more than 100 posts, newer posts beyond the 100-limit will never appear in the feed at all, silently truncating feed coverage.

The SITE_URL is hardcoded to 'https://www.saltgrassmodular.com' (line 4), not pulled from an env var. If the site is ever redeployed under a different domain or staging URL, all RSS item links and the self-referencing atom:link will point to the wrong origin.
No caching headers are set on the Response. Every RSS reader poll triggers a fresh fetch to the Railway backend. Consider adding Cache-Control: max-age=3600 or similar to reduce upstream load.

discoveredConventions.name: PUBLIC_API_URL + PUBLIC_LOCATION_ID env pattern
discoveredConventions.evidence: getBlogPosts() in lib/blog.ts uses import.meta.env.PUBLIC_API_URL with Railway fallback and PUBLIC_LOCATION_ID — same pattern as health.ts / s.ts / t.ts

files.purpose: FILE DOES NOT EXIST in this repository. The path apps/saltgrass-modular/src/pages/compare/[slug].astro was listed for extraction but is not present on disk.

findings.note: File does not exist. The compare/ directory is absent entirely from apps/saltgrass-modular/src/pages/. This may be a planned but unbuilt comparison-page feature.

files.purpose: FILE DOES NOT EXIST in this repository. The path apps/saltgrass-modular/src/pages/compare/index.astro was listed for extraction but is not present on disk.

findings.note: File does not exist. The compare/ directory is absent entirely from apps/saltgrass-modular/src/pages/.

files.purpose: Contact page for Saltgrass Modular that presents phone/email/address contact options and hosts a tabbed interface with two embedded forms (QuoteRequestForm and ConsultationForm) plus a placeholder LPAI calendar booking widget.

connectionsOut.detail: Submitted by QuoteRequestForm client-side script on form submit; honeypot field website_url is silently dropped
Submitted by ConsultationForm client-side script on form submit; honeypot field website_url is silently dropped

exposesEndpoints.detail: Publicly accessible static contact page rendered at build time.

findings.note: The LPAI calendar widget at line 100-113 is a placeholder stub — it renders a text message stating the calendar will be configured during account setup. No actual embed or iframe exists. Visitors who scroll to 'Schedule Your Consultation' see no booking UI, only instructional copy. This is a conversion gap.

The LPAI location ID 'plan_chNIQv7tfy09Ew_h1liV' is hardcoded as a fallback default inside both QuoteRequestForm.astro (line 3) and ConsultationForm.astro (line 3) and also visible in the HTML source at contact.astro line 110 as a <code> element. While locationId is a tenant identifier not a secret, its public exposure and hardcoding in two places creates a drift risk if the location migrates.

Both form submit handlers (QuoteRequestForm + ConsultationForm) perform the honeypot check then silently show the success state without actually submitting — a bot that fills the honeypot gets a false-positive success UX. This is intentional anti-bot behavior but worth noting for support debugging ('I submitted a form but nothing came in').

The tab switcher JavaScript at line 148 uses onclick= inline handlers (switchTab('quote')) which are declared as a plain function in a script block. In strict Astro environments with Content Security Policy, inline onclick handlers may be blocked. The function is not attached via addEventListener, making it incompatible with strict CSP setups.

discoveredConventions.name: Honeypot bot-protection pattern on all forms
discoveredConventions.evidence: Both QuoteRequestForm and ConsultationForm include a visually-hidden input name='website_url' with aria-hidden and position:absolute;left:-9999px. The submit handler checks fd.get('website_url') and silently succeeds without posting if filled.

discoveredConventions.name: LPAI portal forms API as form submission target
discoveredConventions.evidence: Both forms POST to /api/portal/forms/<slug>/submit on the LPAI Railway backend with { locationId, data, meta } shape

files.purpose: FILE DOES NOT EXIST in this repository. The path apps/saltgrass-modular/src/pages/cost/[slug].astro was listed for extraction but is not present on disk.

findings.note: File does not exist. The cost/ directory is absent entirely from apps/saltgrass-modular/src/pages/. This may be a planned but unbuilt cost-guide programmatic SEO feature.

files.purpose: Static marketing page targeting real estate developers, government contractors, real estate investors, and educational institutions, presenting Saltgrass Modular's volume production capabilities, RFQ process, competitive advantages, a case study highlight, and FAQ section.

connectionsOut.detail: Page shell layout with SEO meta tags
Bottom CTA banner linking to /contact for RFQ submission

exposesEndpoints.detail: Fully static SSG page, no dynamic data fetching. No auth required.

findings.note: The page claims '50+ completed projects' and '99% client satisfaction' (lines 72-78) as hard-coded marketing copy. These figures are not sourced from a CMS or backend and will silently become stale as the business grows without a code change.

The case study at lines 270-302 is a generic placeholder ('12-Unit Multi-Family Development, Oklahoma') with no real client name, photos, or links. The testimonial is attributed only to '— Residential Developer'. If Saltgrass has real case studies, this should pull from the LPAI backend or a data file to be credible to B2B prospects.

The page is titled 'For Developers & Government' and the hero targets developers broadly, but the URL is /developers (not /developers-government or /enterprise). The /services/developers.astro page also exists — these may compete for the same SEO keywords and visitor intent.

There is a /services/developers.astro file at apps/saltgrass-modular/src/pages/services/developers.astro serving a similar audience. Having two developer-targeted pages (/developers and /services/developers) without clear differentiation may fragment link equity and confuse navigation.

discoveredConventions.name: Astro component composition pattern
discoveredConventions.evidence: developers.astro uses BaseLayout + Hero + CtaBanner as composable section components — same pattern as about.astro and other saltgrass-modular pages

discoveredConventions.name: BaseLayout accepts keywords prop for SEO
discoveredConventions.evidence: Line 11 passes keywords='modular development, developer solutions, multi-unit housing, volume production' to BaseLayout — first file in this batch to use the keywords prop, suggesting BaseLayout renders a <meta name=keywords> tag

files.purpose: Static Astro page for the Saltgrass Modular financing hub. Presents three financing options (construction loans via Hearth, cash payment plans with milestone tables, HELOC), a FAQ accordion, and a CTA section that opens the consultation modal defined on the index page via inline onclick.

connectionsOut.detail: Outbound link to Hearth third-party lender pre-qualification page; rel=noopener noreferrer

exposesEndpoints.detail: Static Astro page — no server-side auth or params

findings.note: The 'Schedule Call' CTA on line 299-303 uses an inline onclick targeting 'consultation-modal' which is only rendered in index.astro. If a user navigates directly to /financing the modal DOM element will not exist and the button will silently fail.
The Hearth link opens externally with no tracking/UTM parameters, so conversions from this page cannot be attributed in analytics.

discoveredConventions.name: Inline onclick modal trigger pattern
discoveredConventions.evidence: Line 299: onclick directly calls document.getElementById('consultation-modal')?.classList.remove('hidden') — same pattern as index.astro's CTA buttons

files.purpose: FILE DOES NOT EXIST in the repository. No glossary.astro was found under apps/saltgrass-modular/src/pages/.

findings.note: File does not exist at the specified path. The task listed it but the filesystem confirms it is absent.

files.purpose: Static Astro page targeting residential homeowner buyers. Covers modular home value props, popular model showcases (with prices), the customer journey timeline, four financing option cards, testimonial quotes, and a FAQ accordion using native HTML details/summary elements.

connectionsOut.detail: Imported but not used in template — potential dead import

exposesEndpoints.detail: Static Astro page — no auth, no dynamic data

findings.note: ServicesGrid is imported (line 4) but never rendered in the template body. Dead import.

Hearth description on this page says 'funds in 24 hours / pre-qualify in 2 min' (lines 176-178) while financing.astro says 'pre-qualify in minutes / approval 2-4 weeks'. Minor copy inconsistency across pages that may confuse buyers comparing the two.

Native HTML <details>/<summary> accordion pattern used for FAQ (lines 257-315). This is a different UI pattern from the rest of the site which uses custom JS components — no visual consistency enforcement.

discoveredConventions.name: Native HTML details/summary accordion for FAQ
discoveredConventions.evidence: Lines 257-315 use <details class='...' group> with Tailwind group-open utilities rather than a JS-driven accordion — a progressive-enhancement pattern

files.purpose: Home page / entry point of the Saltgrass Modular Astro site. Renders the Hero with dual CTAs, ServicesGrid, StatsStrip, CtaBanner, plus two modal overlays (quote request and consultation) wired by a client-side script that listens for data-action attributes.

exposesEndpoints.detail: Static Astro home page — no server-side auth

findings.note: Modals (quote-modal, consultation-modal) are only rendered in index.astro DOM. Other pages (financing.astro, and possibly others) trigger these modals via inline onclick or data-action, but those modal divs won't exist unless the user landed on / first. This is a cross-page modal coupling issue.

The data-action='quote-modal' dispatch pattern in the client <script> (lines 82-101) uses document.getElementById which returns null if the element is missing — fails silently. No fallback to a full /contact page is provided.

discoveredConventions.name: data-action modal trigger pattern
discoveredConventions.evidence: Lines 87-90: querySelectorAll('[data-action="modalId"]') iterates all triggers and attaches click→classList.remove('hidden'). Allows any element anywhere in the page to open a named modal without inline JS.

discoveredConventions.name: Modal outside-click dismiss
discoveredConventions.evidence: Lines 94-98: listens on the modal backdrop div itself; closes when e.target.id === modalId, meaning clicks on the backdrop (not the inner content) close the modal.

files.purpose: FILE DOES NOT EXIST in the repository. No locations/ directory was found under apps/saltgrass-modular/src/pages/.

findings.note: File does not exist at the specified path. The task listed it but the filesystem confirms it is absent.

files.purpose: FILE DOES NOT EXIST in the repository. No locations/ directory was found under apps/saltgrass-modular/src/pages/.

findings.note: File does not exist at the specified path. The task listed it but the filesystem confirms it is absent.

files.purpose: Static marketing page targeting military and government buyers, showcasing FEMA/GSA certifications, government use-case applications, rapid-deployment scenarios, and logistical capabilities. Provides direct phone and email CTAs for Dylan Walker rather than a form.

connectionsOut.detail: Page shell with title/description/keywords SEO props
Direct phone link to Dylan Walker — no form submission path
Direct email link to Dylan Walker

exposesEndpoints.detail: Static Astro page, no dynamic segments, SSG

findings.note: Contact CTAs bypass the /contact form entirely — phone and email links are hardcoded to Dylan Walker's personal contact info. If the contact owner changes, this page requires a manual code edit. No LPAI backend contact capture occurs for government/military leads from this page.
Keywords prop is passed to BaseLayout (line 10) but container-homes.astro and pools.astro do not pass keywords — inconsistency across sibling pages in whether the keywords SEO prop is used.

The hero image references /img/military-hero.jpg (line 17) — this is a static asset path; if the file does not exist in public/img/ it will be a 404 at runtime. Same pattern exists on process.astro and projects.astro.

discoveredConventions.name: Direct contact CTA pattern (phone + email) for specialty verticals
discoveredConventions.evidence: military.astro has a dedicated 'Ready to Deploy?' section with tel: and mailto: links to Dylan Walker rather than routing to /contact or a form embed — same as homeowners.astro likely does for its CTA

discoveredConventions.name: BaseLayout keywords prop usage is inconsistent across pages
discoveredConventions.evidence: military.astro passes keywords prop; container-homes.astro and pools.astro do not — no enforced contract at the component level

files.purpose: Static product catalog page listing four container home models (320 SF studio, 640 SF 1-bed, 960 SF 2-bed, custom) with pricing, features, and specs. Renders entirely from a hardcoded in-file data array — no API calls.

exposesEndpoints.detail: Static Astro SSG page, no dynamic segments

findings.note: 'Request Quote' buttons (lines 157) use btn-primary class but have no onclick handler, href, or form trigger — clicking them does nothing. The quote CTA is dead UI.
All model pricing and specs are hardcoded in the Astro frontmatter array (lines 6-75). There is no CMS or API backing this data — any price or spec change requires a code deploy.
Hero image references /img/models-hero.jpg (line 87) — static asset with no fallback; 404 if missing from public/img/.

discoveredConventions.name: Hardcoded frontmatter data arrays for product catalogs
discoveredConventions.evidence: models array defined in Astro frontmatter (lines 6-75) drives the entire page render — no fetch to LPAI backend for product data, unlike blog/[slug].astro which calls the API

discoveredConventions.name: Dead CTA button pattern
discoveredConventions.evidence: 'Request Quote' buttons exist in both container-homes.astro and pools.astro with no action wired — consistent across model catalog pages

files.purpose: Static product catalog page for modular pool models, presenting two construction types (tube steel + fiberglass, welded steel + epoxy) each in three sizes, with pricing, add-ons, and inclusions. Includes a client-side smooth-scroll function and hardcoded data arrays.

exposesEndpoints.detail: Static Astro SSG page with inline client-side JS for smooth-scroll

findings.note: 'Request Quote' buttons (lines 104, 163) use btn-primary class but have no onclick, href, or form action — same dead CTA pattern as container-homes.astro.

scrollToSection() is defined in a <script> block (line 284) and called via inline onclick attributes (lines 44, 49). This is a global function attached to window — works but could collide if other scripts define the same name. Astro's default script scoping does NOT apply to functions called via inline onclick.
All pool model data (tubeSteelModels, weldedSteelModels arrays lines 6-16) is hardcoded — same no-CMS pattern as container-homes.astro. Price changes require code deploys.

discoveredConventions.name: Inline onclick + global window function for client-side interactivity
discoveredConventions.evidence: scrollToSection() defined in <script> block called via onclick attribute strings — bypasses Astro's module scoping, a pattern that works but is fragile

files.purpose: FILE NOT FOUND. privacy.astro does not exist in apps/saltgrass-modular/src/pages/ or any subdirectory. The file was listed for extraction but is absent from the repository at HEAD.

findings.note: privacy.astro does not exist in the saltgrass-modular app. If a /privacy route is expected (e.g. linked from footer or required by Stripe/LPAI compliance), it is missing entirely.

files.purpose: Static informational page describing Saltgrass Modular's 7-stage build process (Discovery through Handoff) with timelines, activity checklists, FAQ section, and an aggregate timeline summary. All data is hardcoded in the Astro frontmatter.

connectionsOut.detail: Page shell with title/description SEO props

exposesEndpoints.detail: Static Astro SSG page, no dynamic segments or client JS

findings.note: The page title says '8-Stage Process' (line 102) and the Hero headline repeats 'Our 8-Stage Process' (line 106), but the stages array contains exactly 7 entries (Discovery through Handoff, lines 8-97). The count in copy is wrong.

The FAQ mentions 'See our Financing page' (line 228) without a hyperlink — the text assumes a /financing route exists (which it does in this app) but the cross-link is plain text, reducing discoverability.

discoveredConventions.name: Hardcoded frontmatter data arrays for process/stages content
discoveredConventions.evidence: stages array (lines 6-97) drives the entire timeline visualization and detail cards — no CMS fetch, same pattern as model catalog pages

files.purpose: Static portfolio/case-study page displaying 6 completed projects (3 residential homes, 2 pools, 1 commercial development) in a filterable grid with testimonials and aggregate project statistics. Includes client-side JS for category filter toggling.

connectionsOut.detail: Page shell with title/description SEO props

exposesEndpoints.detail: Static Astro SSG page with inline client-side JS filter

findings.note: 'See Details' buttons (line 204) use btn-secondary with no href or onclick — clicking does nothing. No project detail pages exist to route to.

filterProjects() (line 303) references the implicit global `event` object rather than receiving it as a parameter. This is a browser-specific implicit global that is deprecated and not available in all contexts. Should be `onclick='filterProjects(event, "...")' function filterProjects(event, category)'.

Category filter onclick (line 163) uses template literal string `filterProjects('${cat.id}')` but the handler references implicit `event.target` (line 303) — if the click registers on a child element of the button, event.target may not be the button itself and the class toggle will fail.

All 6 project entries use placeholder CSS gradient colors for images (e.g. 'gradient-to-br from-blue-400 to-blue-600') instead of real photos (line 19). The image field is consumed as a Tailwind class string appended to bg-gradient-to-br — this works but means real photos would require changing data structure, not just swapping a src.

discoveredConventions.name: Client-side DOM filter pattern via global onclick + data-category attributes
discoveredConventions.evidence: filterProjects() reads data-category from card elements and toggles display:none/block — same inline-onclick-global-function pattern as pools.astro scrollToSection()

discoveredConventions.name: Gradient string as image placeholder in data arrays
discoveredConventions.evidence: projects array stores Tailwind gradient class strings in the image field (e.g. 'gradient-to-br from-blue-400 to-blue-600') used as bg- class suffix — signals real photos not yet integrated

files.purpose: Dynamic QR-code redirect handler. At SSR request time it calls the LPAI backend to resolve a short code to a redirectUrl, then issues a 302 redirect. Falls through to a 404 response if the backend returns non-OK or throws.

connectionsOut.detail: Resolves QR code to redirectUrl; API_BASE defaults to Railway NestJS URL

exposesEndpoints.detail: SSR dynamic route; no auth guard on the page itself — auth is at the backend

findings.note: The `code` param is passed through encodeURIComponent before being interpolated into the fetch URL, which is correct. However, no length or character-set validation is applied before the fetch — an arbitrarily long code value would still reach the backend.

The catch block is a bare no-op; any network error (Railway down, DNS failure) silently returns 404 to the user with no logging, making it impossible to distinguish a bad QR code from an infrastructure outage at the page level.

Location scoping is handled by passing PUBLIC_LOCATION_ID as a query param — this means all QR codes on this site are resolved against a single tenant; cross-tenant QR resolution is not supported by this page.

discoveredConventions.name: PUBLIC_API_URL + PUBLIC_LOCATION_ID env pattern
discoveredConventions.evidence: Both env vars imported at the top of the frontmatter with Railway fallback — same pattern observed in blog, health, and beacon proxy pages

discoveredConventions.name: SSR redirect via Astro.redirect / new Response
discoveredConventions.evidence: File uses `import { redirect } from 'astro'` and `new Response(..., { status: 404 })` — Astro SSR response-termination pattern

files.purpose: Static educational guide detail page for the Saltgrass 101 learning hub. Uses getStaticPaths() to pre-build 8 guide slugs at build time; guide content is hardcoded in-file as a data map, not fetched from the backend.

exposesEndpoints.detail: SSG — pre-built at build time for 8 fixed slugs; unknown slugs redirect to /saltgrass-101

findings.note: The fallback `if (!guide) return Astro.redirect('/saltgrass-101')` on line 113 can never be reached in SSG mode because getStaticPaths() only registers the 8 known slugs — Astro will 404 any unknown slug before the frontmatter runs. The guard is a no-op in static output mode.

All guide content (titles, descriptions, section text, pricing figures) is hardcoded in the JS object on lines 21-110. Any content update requires a code change and redeploy rather than a CMS update — this will become friction as guide content evolves.
The hero image path `/img/saltgrass-101-hero.jpg` is shared across all 8 guide slugs with no per-guide hero image — all guide detail pages render identical hero imagery regardless of content.

discoveredConventions.name: Astro static site generation with hardcoded slug manifest
discoveredConventions.evidence: getStaticPaths() returns a literal array of slug objects rather than fetching from backend — content-as-code pattern, unlike blog/[slug].astro which fetches LPAI backend

discoveredConventions.name: Astro component composition pattern
discoveredConventions.evidence: BaseLayout + Hero + CtaBanner used as composable section wrappers — same pattern as about.astro and other page files

files.purpose: Saltgrass 101 learning hub index page. Renders a grid of 8 educational guide cards (each linking to /saltgrass-101/[slug]) and a blog CTA. All content is static — no backend calls.

exposesEndpoints.detail: Fully static index page — no SSR, no auth

findings.note: The guides array on lines 6-55 is a separate definition from the `guides` Record in saltgrass-101/[slug].astro. Both must be manually kept in sync — a slug added to one but not the other will produce a broken card link or an orphaned route.

The hero image `/img/saltgrass-101-hero.jpg` is referenced here and identically in the detail page. If the asset is missing at build time, both index and all 8 detail pages will render broken images silently (no fallback or alt-based degradation beyond the alt attribute).

discoveredConventions.name: Astro component composition pattern
discoveredConventions.evidence: BaseLayout + Hero + CtaBanner used as composable section wrappers — consistent with other page files in this module

files.purpose: Static marketing service page for Saltgrass custom container homes. Renders hero, feature list, spec strip, model gallery with pricing, project showcase, benefits comparison, FAQ, and a CTA banner. All content is hardcoded; no backend calls.

exposesEndpoints.detail: Fully static service page — no SSR, no auth

findings.note: The three 'Request Quote' buttons (lines 123, 138, 152) are plain `<button>` elements with no form, href, JS handler, or event listener attached. Clicking them does nothing. This is a dead CTA.

The 'Recent Projects' gallery section (lines 164-196) uses CSS gradient placeholders (`bg-gradient-to-br from-blue-400 to-blue-600 h-64`) instead of real project photos — these are unfilled placeholder blocks that may ship to production and look unfinished.
The 'Popular Models' gallery (lines 111-159) also uses gradient color blocks (`bg-gradient-to-br`) instead of real product images — same placeholder-shipping risk.

FAQ answer for cost quotes '640 SF one-bedroom ranges from $90K-$120K' (line 33) while the model card on line 137 shows 'Starting at $105K'. The range in the FAQ implies a floor below $105K but the card only shows $105K — minor pricing inconsistency visible to prospects.

discoveredConventions.name: Astro component composition pattern
discoveredConventions.evidence: BaseLayout + Hero + CtaBanner used as composable section wrappers

discoveredConventions.name: Inline data arrays for static content
discoveredConventions.evidence: specs, features, and faqItems defined as JS arrays in frontmatter and mapped in template — reusable without a CMS

files.purpose: Static B2B marketing page targeting developers, military, government agencies, and real estate investors. Covers market segments, the RFQ process (5-step timeline), competitive advantages, and a developer-focused CTA. No backend calls.

exposesEndpoints.detail: Fully static service page — no SSR, no auth

findings.note: The CtaBanner at line 269 prompts developers to 'Submit your RFQ or schedule a developer call' but this page contains no form, no RFQ submission mechanism, no mailto link, and no href to an RFQ form — the CTA has no actionable destination.
The hero image `/img/developers-hero.jpg` is referenced but not verified to exist in the public assets — same silent-broken-image risk as other service pages.

The developer-facing copy references `/financing` and implies government/GSA schedule availability, but no links to those pages appear in the body — a developer landing here has no in-page navigation to supporting documentation.

discoveredConventions.name: Astro component composition pattern
discoveredConventions.evidence: BaseLayout + Hero + CtaBanner used as composable section wrappers

files.purpose: Static marketing page for Saltgrass disaster relief and rapid deployment services. Covers emergency housing types, why-modular rationale, four deployment scenarios, technical specs, compliance certifications, and direct contact CTAs with a phone number and email. No backend calls.

connectionsOut.detail: CTA banner section component — NOT rendered in this file; page ends without a CtaBanner closing call

exposesEndpoints.detail: Fully static service page — no SSR, no auth

findings.note: CtaBanner is imported on line 4 but never rendered in the template — unlike every other service page which ends with a <CtaBanner> component. The page ends with a bespoke inline CTA section (lines 195-212) instead. The import is dead code and the page is structurally inconsistent with sibling pages.

Dylan's direct phone number (405-659-1949) and email address (Dylan.Walker@saltgrassmodular.com) are hardcoded in the template markup (lines 203-207). These are publicly visible in source and at risk of harvesting by scrapers for spam; no obfuscation is applied.
The hero image `/img/disaster-relief-hero.jpg` is referenced but existence of the asset is unverified — same silent-broken-image risk as other pages using placeholder or missing images.

discoveredConventions.name: Astro component composition pattern
discoveredConventions.evidence: BaseLayout + Hero used as composable section wrappers — CtaBanner imported but not used, exposing that the convention is not always followed

discoveredConventions.name: Hardcoded contact info pattern
discoveredConventions.evidence: Phone and email embedded directly in template markup rather than pulled from a config or CMS — same pattern used across saltgrass-modular service pages

files.purpose: File does not exist in the repository. No services/index.astro is present under apps/saltgrass-modular/src/pages/services/ — the directory contains only pools.astro, traditional-builds.astro, container-homes.astro, developers.astro, and disaster-relief.astro.

findings.note: src/pages/services/index.astro does not exist. There is no umbrella services listing page under the /services route for saltgrass-modular. The site homepage (src/pages/index.astro) links to /services via a Hero CTA, which will 404 at runtime since no index route exists under services/.

files.purpose: Static Astro page rendering the Saltgrass Modular pools service detail. Presents two pool product lines (tube steel + fiberglass and welded steel + epoxy), a comparison table, included-items lists, FAQ, and a CTA banner — all fully static with no runtime data fetching.

exposesEndpoints.detail: Astro SSG static page — public, no auth

findings.note: 'Request Quote' buttons (lines 58, 96) are plain <button> elements with no href, form binding, or onclick handler — clicking them does nothing at runtime. No modal wiring, no form submission, no navigation target present in this file.
Hero image references /img/pools-hero.jpg (line 16) — this is a static asset path that must exist in the public/ directory at build time. No fallback or error handling if missing.

The comparison table lists tube steel + fiberglass durability as '8–12 years' (line 123) but the FAQ directly below says '8–12 years with proper maintenance' (line 209) — consistent internally, but a minor UX gap: the warranty callout (lines 39-40) says '2 years frame, 5 years fiberglass' which is far shorter and could cause user confusion.

discoveredConventions.name: Three-import Astro page pattern (BaseLayout + Hero + CtaBanner)
discoveredConventions.evidence: pools.astro imports exactly BaseLayout, Hero, CtaBanner and no other dependencies — same pattern seen in about.astro and traditional-builds.astro

discoveredConventions.name: Fully static service detail pages with no runtime data fetching
discoveredConventions.evidence: No fetch/API call anywhere in the file; all content is hardcoded in the template

files.purpose: Static Astro page for the Saltgrass Modular 'Traditional Custom Builds' service. Presents architectural styles, construction timeline, included-items list, per-SF pricing tiers, FAQ, and a CTA banner — fully static, no runtime data fetching.

exposesEndpoints.detail: Astro SSG static page — public, no auth

findings.note: Hero image references /img/traditional-builds-hero.jpg (line 16) and inline references /img/traditional-home-exterior.jpg (line 52) — both must exist as static assets in public/. No fallback or lazy loading specified on the inline img.
CTA banner (line 195-198) says 'Schedule a consultation' but no link or modal trigger is present in this file. The consultation flow is not wired here — user has no navigation target from this CTA.

The 'Financing page' is referenced as a link destination in two FAQ answers (lines 181, 187) but the anchor is plain text, not an actual <a href> — these are dead references that don't navigate anywhere.

discoveredConventions.name: Three-import Astro page pattern (BaseLayout + Hero + CtaBanner)
discoveredConventions.evidence: traditional-builds.astro imports exactly BaseLayout, Hero, CtaBanner — identical to pools.astro

discoveredConventions.name: Inline .map() for dynamic list rendering in Astro templates
discoveredConventions.evidence: Lines 65-77 (architectural styles), 107-125 (included items) use .map() with JSX-style rendering inside Astro templates

files.purpose: Terms of Service legal page for the LeadProspecting AI marketing site (apps/web-marketing). Renders 18 numbered ToS sections with a sticky desktop sidebar TOC, mobile accordion TOC, back-to-top button, and IntersectionObserver-driven active-section highlighting. Fully static — no API calls.

connectionsOut.detail: Navigation component injected into nav slot
Footer component injected into footer slot

exposesEndpoints.detail: Astro SSG static page — public, no auth. Canonical at leadprospecting.ai/terms

findings.note: Section 9 (Third-Party Integrations, lines 163-189) contains external links to Google, Meta, LinkedIn, and Microsoft developer terms pages as target='_blank' rel='noopener noreferrer'. These links are hardcoded and will silently become stale if third-party ToS URLs change — no automated freshness check exists.

The /privacy link (line 85) and /pricing link (line 102) are relative hrefs — they depend on those routes existing in the same app. If the privacy or pricing pages are missing or at different paths, these links silently 404.

Section 8 explicitly states 'LPAI does not use Your Data... to train generalized AI/ML models' (line 158) and Section 5 states 'The Lead Finder does not use data obtained through Google, Meta, LinkedIn, or Microsoft APIs' (line 137). These are compliance-critical claims — any future backend change that touches AI model training pipelines or scraper data sources must update this ToS page to remain legally accurate.

IntersectionObserver active-TOC logic (lines 322-341) casts HTMLElement as HTMLAnchorElement inline — TypeScript 'as' cast without null guard on getAttribute. Low risk at runtime (elements are always anchors here) but fragile if the sections array changes.
The 'Last updated: May 4, 2026' date (line 35) is hardcoded. If the terms content is edited without updating this date, it becomes a misleading legal timestamp.

discoveredConventions.name: Nav/Footer slot injection pattern in web-marketing
discoveredConventions.evidence: terms.astro passes <Nav slot='nav'> and <Footer slot='footer'> to BaseLayout — distinct from saltgrass-modular which uses no explicit slots

discoveredConventions.name: IntersectionObserver for sticky sidebar TOC active-state
discoveredConventions.evidence: Client-side script (lines 317-341) uses IntersectionObserver with rootMargin '-80px 0px -60% 0px' to highlight the TOC link matching the on-screen section

discoveredConventions.name: Mobile vs desktop TOC via accordion + sticky sidebar
discoveredConventions.evidence: Two parallel TOC implementations: mobile accordion (lines 41-58, JS toggle at 284-302) and desktop sticky sidebar (lines 65-79). Same sections array drives both.

findings.note: All stats (3+ years, 50+ projects, 99% satisfaction) are hardcoded copy. If this data changes, it requires a code edit rather than a CMS update — drift risk over time.
Team section uses placeholder gradient divs instead of actual photos for Engineering Team and Installation Specialists — placeholder state shipped to production.

Images (/img/about-hero.jpg, /img/dylan-portrait.jpg, /img/facility-tour.jpg) are referenced but their existence in /public is not verifiable from this file alone — broken image risk if assets are not deployed.
uptime field is set to startTime (Date.now() at request start) rather than process uptime or a stable timestamp — this will always reflect request-start epoch ms, not actual service uptime.
statusCode logic sets 503 for both 'timeout' and 'error' states — the comment and overallStatus distinguish them but HTTP callers only see 503, losing the timeout signal.
PUBLIC_LOCATION_ID is forwarded to the backend health check to scope the DB probe — correct multi-tenant pattern for a single-location client site.
No auth on this endpoint — intentional for uptime probing, but the response includes database status which could inform an attacker whether the DB is reachable. Low practical risk but worth noting.

Regex rewrite `var u="[^"]+"` assumes the backend tracking script uses a specific variable name/format. If the backend script changes that variable name, the rewrite silently fails and the beacon URL is not patched — tracking breaks without any error signal.

On backend error, returns HTTP 204 with empty body and Content-Type: application/javascript. Browsers will execute the empty response silently — no JS error but tracking is fully dead. Silent failure is intentional per comment.
Script is cached 1 hour (max-age=3600). If the backend tracking script is updated, cached clients will not pick it up for up to an hour.
No validation or rate-limiting on the proxy — any client can POST arbitrary payloads to the backend tracking endpoint via this relay. The backend must enforce its own validation and rate limits.

Content-Type is hardcoded as 'application/json' when forwarding to backend, but request.text() reads the raw body regardless of incoming Content-Type. If the tracking script sends non-JSON, the backend may reject it.
res.body is streamed directly as the response. If the backend returns a streaming or chunked response, edge-runtime stream piping behavior in Astro SSR may vary.

chrome.headerHtml, chrome.footerHtml, and post.content are all rendered with set:html (raw HTML injection). If the backend returns malicious HTML (e.g. from a compromised CMS record), it executes in the browser. Trust is entirely in the backend/admin data.

CTA gradient detection: `ctaIsTailwindGradient` checks if gradient starts with 'from-' to distinguish Tailwind class from inline CSS. If a Tailwind gradient class doesn't start with 'from-' (e.g. 'bg-gradient-to-r from-...'), the detection fails and ctaInlineBg is set incorrectly.
getStaticPaths calls getBlogPosts() which uses limit=100. Sites with >100 posts will silently drop slugs from the pre-rendered set — those posts will 404 at runtime.
All API calls correctly scope to PUBLIC_LOCATION_ID — correct single-tenant isolation for this client site.

On each page build, 5 separate API calls are made (posts list, post by slug, related posts, CTAs, chrome settings). Promise.all parallelizes 3 of them but the initial getBlogPosts() in getStaticPaths runs sequentially before the detail rendering phase.
getBlogPosts() uses limit=100. If the location has >100 posts, the listing silently truncates — no pagination UI is rendered. The filter/search only operates on the pre-fetched 100-post window.

Featured post selection: `posts.find(p => p.heroStyle === 'featured') || posts[0]`. If no post has heroStyle='featured', the newest post (posts[0]) is featured by API return order — this is implicit and may surprise content editors.

Client-side category and search filtering operates on data-attributes injected at build time (data-cat, data-title, data-excerpt). All filtering is zero-latency JS with no network round-trips — good pattern.

Category fallback logic derives categories from post data if API returns empty array. The fallback uses post.category values directly as both slug and name, so category slugs will not be URL-safe if post.category contains spaces or special chars.
Both API calls correctly scope to PUBLIC_LOCATION_ID — correct single-tenant isolation for this client site.

getBlogPosts() fetches up to 100 posts from the backend (limit=100) but rss.xml.ts only renders the first 30 (.slice(0, 30)). If there are more than 100 posts, newer posts beyond the 100-limit will never appear in the feed at all, silently truncating feed coverage.

The SITE_URL is hardcoded to 'https://www.saltgrassmodular.com' (line 4), not pulled from an env var. If the site is ever redeployed under a different domain or staging URL, all RSS item links and the self-referencing atom:link will point to the wrong origin.
No caching headers are set on the Response. Every RSS reader poll triggers a fresh fetch to the Railway backend. Consider adding Cache-Control: max-age=3600 or similar to reduce upstream load.
File does not exist. The compare/ directory is absent entirely from apps/saltgrass-modular/src/pages/. This may be a planned but unbuilt comparison-page feature.
File does not exist. The compare/ directory is absent entirely from apps/saltgrass-modular/src/pages/.

The LPAI calendar widget at line 100-113 is a placeholder stub — it renders a text message stating the calendar will be configured during account setup. No actual embed or iframe exists. Visitors who scroll to 'Schedule Your Consultation' see no booking UI, only instructional copy. This is a conversion gap.

The LPAI location ID 'plan_chNIQv7tfy09Ew_h1liV' is hardcoded as a fallback default inside both QuoteRequestForm.astro (line 3) and ConsultationForm.astro (line 3) and also visible in the HTML source at contact.astro line 110 as a <code> element. While locationId is a tenant identifier not a secret, its public exposure and hardcoding in two places creates a drift risk if the location migrates.

Both form submit handlers (QuoteRequestForm + ConsultationForm) perform the honeypot check then silently show the success state without actually submitting — a bot that fills the honeypot gets a false-positive success UX. This is intentional anti-bot behavior but worth noting for support debugging ('I submitted a form but nothing came in').

The tab switcher JavaScript at line 148 uses onclick= inline handlers (switchTab('quote')) which are declared as a plain function in a script block. In strict Astro environments with Content Security Policy, inline onclick handlers may be blocked. The function is not attached via addEventListener, making it incompatible with strict CSP setups.
File does not exist. The cost/ directory is absent entirely from apps/saltgrass-modular/src/pages/. This may be a planned but unbuilt cost-guide programmatic SEO feature.

The page claims '50+ completed projects' and '99% client satisfaction' (lines 72-78) as hard-coded marketing copy. These figures are not sourced from a CMS or backend and will silently become stale as the business grows without a code change.

The case study at lines 270-302 is a generic placeholder ('12-Unit Multi-Family Development, Oklahoma') with no real client name, photos, or links. The testimonial is attributed only to '— Residential Developer'. If Saltgrass has real case studies, this should pull from the LPAI backend or a data file to be credible to B2B prospects.

The page is titled 'For Developers & Government' and the hero targets developers broadly, but the URL is /developers (not /developers-government or /enterprise). The /services/developers.astro page also exists — these may compete for the same SEO keywords and visitor intent.

There is a /services/developers.astro file at apps/saltgrass-modular/src/pages/services/developers.astro serving a similar audience. Having two developer-targeted pages (/developers and /services/developers) without clear differentiation may fragment link equity and confuse navigation.

The 'Schedule Call' CTA on line 299-303 uses an inline onclick targeting 'consultation-modal' which is only rendered in index.astro. If a user navigates directly to /financing the modal DOM element will not exist and the button will silently fail.
The Hearth link opens externally with no tracking/UTM parameters, so conversions from this page cannot be attributed in analytics.
File does not exist at the specified path. The task listed it but the filesystem confirms it is absent.
ServicesGrid is imported (line 4) but never rendered in the template body. Dead import.

Hearth description on this page says 'funds in 24 hours / pre-qualify in 2 min' (lines 176-178) while financing.astro says 'pre-qualify in minutes / approval 2-4 weeks'. Minor copy inconsistency across pages that may confuse buyers comparing the two.

Native HTML <details>/<summary> accordion pattern used for FAQ (lines 257-315). This is a different UI pattern from the rest of the site which uses custom JS components — no visual consistency enforcement.

Modals (quote-modal, consultation-modal) are only rendered in index.astro DOM. Other pages (financing.astro, and possibly others) trigger these modals via inline onclick or data-action, but those modal divs won't exist unless the user landed on / first. This is a cross-page modal coupling issue.

The data-action='quote-modal' dispatch pattern in the client <script> (lines 82-101) uses document.getElementById which returns null if the element is missing — fails silently. No fallback to a full /contact page is provided.
File does not exist at the specified path. The task listed it but the filesystem confirms it is absent.
File does not exist at the specified path. The task listed it but the filesystem confirms it is absent.

Contact CTAs bypass the /contact form entirely — phone and email links are hardcoded to Dylan Walker's personal contact info. If the contact owner changes, this page requires a manual code edit. No LPAI backend contact capture occurs for government/military leads from this page.
Keywords prop is passed to BaseLayout (line 10) but container-homes.astro and pools.astro do not pass keywords — inconsistency across sibling pages in whether the keywords SEO prop is used.

The hero image references /img/military-hero.jpg (line 17) — this is a static asset path; if the file does not exist in public/img/ it will be a 404 at runtime. Same pattern exists on process.astro and projects.astro.
'Request Quote' buttons (lines 157) use btn-primary class but have no onclick handler, href, or form trigger — clicking them does nothing. The quote CTA is dead UI.
All model pricing and specs are hardcoded in the Astro frontmatter array (lines 6-75). There is no CMS or API backing this data — any price or spec change requires a code deploy.
Hero image references /img/models-hero.jpg (line 87) — static asset with no fallback; 404 if missing from public/img/.
'Request Quote' buttons (lines 104, 163) use btn-primary class but have no onclick, href, or form action — same dead CTA pattern as container-homes.astro.

scrollToSection() is defined in a <script> block (line 284) and called via inline onclick attributes (lines 44, 49). This is a global function attached to window — works but could collide if other scripts define the same name. Astro's default script scoping does NOT apply to functions called via inline onclick.
All pool model data (tubeSteelModels, weldedSteelModels arrays lines 6-16) is hardcoded — same no-CMS pattern as container-homes.astro. Price changes require code deploys.
privacy.astro does not exist in the saltgrass-modular app. If a /privacy route is expected (e.g. linked from footer or required by Stripe/LPAI compliance), it is missing entirely.

The page title says '8-Stage Process' (line 102) and the Hero headline repeats 'Our 8-Stage Process' (line 106), but the stages array contains exactly 7 entries (Discovery through Handoff, lines 8-97). The count in copy is wrong.

The FAQ mentions 'See our Financing page' (line 228) without a hyperlink — the text assumes a /financing route exists (which it does in this app) but the cross-link is plain text, reducing discoverability.
'See Details' buttons (line 204) use btn-secondary with no href or onclick — clicking does nothing. No project detail pages exist to route to.

filterProjects() (line 303) references the implicit global `event` object rather than receiving it as a parameter. This is a browser-specific implicit global that is deprecated and not available in all contexts. Should be `onclick='filterProjects(event, "...")' function filterProjects(event, category)'.

Category filter onclick (line 163) uses template literal string `filterProjects('${cat.id}')` but the handler references implicit `event.target` (line 303) — if the click registers on a child element of the button, event.target may not be the button itself and the class toggle will fail.

All 6 project entries use placeholder CSS gradient colors for images (e.g. 'gradient-to-br from-blue-400 to-blue-600') instead of real photos (line 19). The image field is consumed as a Tailwind class string appended to bg-gradient-to-br — this works but means real photos would require changing data structure, not just swapping a src.

The `code` param is passed through encodeURIComponent before being interpolated into the fetch URL, which is correct. However, no length or character-set validation is applied before the fetch — an arbitrarily long code value would still reach the backend.

The catch block is a bare no-op; any network error (Railway down, DNS failure) silently returns 404 to the user with no logging, making it impossible to distinguish a bad QR code from an infrastructure outage at the page level.

Location scoping is handled by passing PUBLIC_LOCATION_ID as a query param — this means all QR codes on this site are resolved against a single tenant; cross-tenant QR resolution is not supported by this page.

The fallback `if (!guide) return Astro.redirect('/saltgrass-101')` on line 113 can never be reached in SSG mode because getStaticPaths() only registers the 8 known slugs — Astro will 404 any unknown slug before the frontmatter runs. The guard is a no-op in static output mode.

All guide content (titles, descriptions, section text, pricing figures) is hardcoded in the JS object on lines 21-110. Any content update requires a code change and redeploy rather than a CMS update — this will become friction as guide content evolves.
The hero image path `/img/saltgrass-101-hero.jpg` is shared across all 8 guide slugs with no per-guide hero image — all guide detail pages render identical hero imagery regardless of content.

The guides array on lines 6-55 is a separate definition from the `guides` Record in saltgrass-101/[slug].astro. Both must be manually kept in sync — a slug added to one but not the other will produce a broken card link or an orphaned route.

The hero image `/img/saltgrass-101-hero.jpg` is referenced here and identically in the detail page. If the asset is missing at build time, both index and all 8 detail pages will render broken images silently (no fallback or alt-based degradation beyond the alt attribute).
The three 'Request Quote' buttons (lines 123, 138, 152) are plain `<button>` elements with no form, href, JS handler, or event listener attached. Clicking them does nothing. This is a dead CTA.

The 'Recent Projects' gallery section (lines 164-196) uses CSS gradient placeholders (`bg-gradient-to-br from-blue-400 to-blue-600 h-64`) instead of real project photos — these are unfilled placeholder blocks that may ship to production and look unfinished.
The 'Popular Models' gallery (lines 111-159) also uses gradient color blocks (`bg-gradient-to-br`) instead of real product images — same placeholder-shipping risk.

FAQ answer for cost quotes '640 SF one-bedroom ranges from $90K-$120K' (line 33) while the model card on line 137 shows 'Starting at $105K'. The range in the FAQ implies a floor below $105K but the card only shows $105K — minor pricing inconsistency visible to prospects.

The CtaBanner at line 269 prompts developers to 'Submit your RFQ or schedule a developer call' but this page contains no form, no RFQ submission mechanism, no mailto link, and no href to an RFQ form — the CTA has no actionable destination.
The hero image `/img/developers-hero.jpg` is referenced but not verified to exist in the public assets — same silent-broken-image risk as other service pages.

The developer-facing copy references `/financing` and implies government/GSA schedule availability, but no links to those pages appear in the body — a developer landing here has no in-page navigation to supporting documentation.

CtaBanner is imported on line 4 but never rendered in the template — unlike every other service page which ends with a <CtaBanner> component. The page ends with a bespoke inline CTA section (lines 195-212) instead. The import is dead code and the page is structurally inconsistent with sibling pages.

Dylan's direct phone number (405-659-1949) and email address (Dylan.Walker@saltgrassmodular.com) are hardcoded in the template markup (lines 203-207). These are publicly visible in source and at risk of harvesting by scrapers for spam; no obfuscation is applied.
The hero image `/img/disaster-relief-hero.jpg` is referenced but existence of the asset is unverified — same silent-broken-image risk as other pages using placeholder or missing images.

src/pages/services/index.astro does not exist. There is no umbrella services listing page under the /services route for saltgrass-modular. The site homepage (src/pages/index.astro) links to /services via a Hero CTA, which will 404 at runtime since no index route exists under services/.

'Request Quote' buttons (lines 58, 96) are plain <button> elements with no href, form binding, or onclick handler — clicking them does nothing at runtime. No modal wiring, no form submission, no navigation target present in this file.
Hero image references /img/pools-hero.jpg (line 16) — this is a static asset path that must exist in the public/ directory at build time. No fallback or error handling if missing.

glossary.name: Astro component composition pattern
glossary.evidence: Uses BaseLayout + Hero + CtaBanner as composable section components — consistent pattern likely repo-wide for Saltgrass Modular pages

glossary.name: PUBLIC_API_URL + PUBLIC_LOCATION_ID env pattern
glossary.evidence: Both vars referenced at top of file as Astro env imports with Railway fallback URL — consistent pattern across all saltgrass-modular API routes

glossary.name: Silent-fail / empty-response on error
glossary.evidence: AbortError caught and returns degraded status rather than throwing — same silent-fail convention seen in s.ts and t.ts

glossary.name: Same-origin proxy for ad-blocker bypass
glossary.evidence: Rewrites tracking URL from absolute backend URL to /api/t — repo-wide pattern for LPAI client sites to bypass ad-blocker domain blacklists

glossary.name: BlogPosting JSON-LD schema injection
glossary.evidence: Inline <script type='application/ld+json'> with BlogPosting schema including headline, author, publisher, datePublished, wordCount — likely a repo-wide pattern for blog detail pages

glossary.name: astro:page-load event listener pattern
glossary.evidence: Client-side scripts initialize on both DOMContentLoaded and astro:page-load to handle Astro view transitions

glossary.name: Build-time data-attribute injection for client-side filtering
glossary.evidence: data-cat / data-title / data-excerpt injected on each post card element, enabling zero-latency JS filtering without any client-side API calls

glossary.name: Honeypot bot-protection pattern on all forms
glossary.evidence: Both QuoteRequestForm and ConsultationForm include a visually-hidden input name='website_url' with aria-hidden and position:absolute;left:-9999px. The submit handler checks fd.get('website_url') and silently succeeds without posting if filled.

glossary.name: LPAI portal forms API as form submission target
glossary.evidence: Both forms POST to /api/portal/forms/<slug>/submit on the LPAI Railway backend with { locationId, data, meta } shape

glossary.name: BaseLayout accepts keywords prop for SEO
glossary.evidence: Line 11 passes keywords='modular development, developer solutions, multi-unit housing, volume production' to BaseLayout — first file in this batch to use the keywords prop, suggesting BaseLayout renders a <meta name=keywords> tag

glossary.name: Inline onclick modal trigger pattern
glossary.evidence: Line 299: onclick directly calls document.getElementById('consultation-modal')?.classList.remove('hidden') — same pattern as index.astro's CTA buttons

glossary.name: Native HTML details/summary accordion for FAQ
glossary.evidence: Lines 257-315 use <details class='...' group> with Tailwind group-open utilities rather than a JS-driven accordion — a progressive-enhancement pattern

glossary.name: data-action modal trigger pattern
glossary.evidence: Lines 87-90: querySelectorAll('[data-action="modalId"]') iterates all triggers and attaches click→classList.remove('hidden'). Allows any element anywhere in the page to open a named modal without inline JS.

glossary.name: Modal outside-click dismiss
glossary.evidence: Lines 94-98: listens on the modal backdrop div itself; closes when e.target.id === modalId, meaning clicks on the backdrop (not the inner content) close the modal.

glossary.name: Direct contact CTA pattern (phone + email) for specialty verticals
glossary.evidence: military.astro has a dedicated 'Ready to Deploy?' section with tel: and mailto: links to Dylan Walker rather than routing to /contact or a form embed — same as homeowners.astro likely does for its CTA

glossary.name: BaseLayout keywords prop usage is inconsistent across pages
glossary.evidence: military.astro passes keywords prop; container-homes.astro and pools.astro do not — no enforced contract at the component level

glossary.name: Hardcoded frontmatter data arrays for product catalogs
glossary.evidence: models array defined in Astro frontmatter (lines 6-75) drives the entire page render — no fetch to LPAI backend for product data, unlike blog/[slug].astro which calls the API

glossary.name: Dead CTA button pattern
glossary.evidence: 'Request Quote' buttons exist in both container-homes.astro and pools.astro with no action wired — consistent across model catalog pages

glossary.name: Inline onclick + global window function for client-side interactivity
glossary.evidence: scrollToSection() defined in <script> block called via onclick attribute strings — bypasses Astro's module scoping, a pattern that works but is fragile

glossary.name: Hardcoded frontmatter data arrays for process/stages content
glossary.evidence: stages array (lines 6-97) drives the entire timeline visualization and detail cards — no CMS fetch, same pattern as model catalog pages

glossary.name: Client-side DOM filter pattern via global onclick + data-category attributes
glossary.evidence: filterProjects() reads data-category from card elements and toggles display:none/block — same inline-onclick-global-function pattern as pools.astro scrollToSection()

glossary.name: Gradient string as image placeholder in data arrays
glossary.evidence: projects array stores Tailwind gradient class strings in the image field (e.g. 'gradient-to-br from-blue-400 to-blue-600') used as bg- class suffix — signals real photos not yet integrated

glossary.name: SSR redirect via Astro.redirect / new Response
glossary.evidence: File uses `import { redirect } from 'astro'` and `new Response(..., { status: 404 })` — Astro SSR response-termination pattern

glossary.name: Astro static site generation with hardcoded slug manifest
glossary.evidence: getStaticPaths() returns a literal array of slug objects rather than fetching from backend — content-as-code pattern, unlike blog/[slug].astro which fetches LPAI backend

glossary.name: Inline data arrays for static content
glossary.evidence: specs, features, and faqItems defined as JS arrays in frontmatter and mapped in template — reusable without a CMS

glossary.name: Hardcoded contact info pattern
glossary.evidence: Phone and email embedded directly in template markup rather than pulled from a config or CMS — same pattern used across saltgrass-modular service pages

glossary.name: Three-import Astro page pattern (BaseLayout + Hero + CtaBanner)
glossary.evidence: pools.astro imports exactly BaseLayout, Hero, CtaBanner and no other dependencies — same pattern seen in about.astro and traditional-builds.astro

glossary.name: Fully static service detail pages with no runtime data fetching
glossary.evidence: No fetch/API call anywhere in the file; all content is hardcoded in the template

glossary.name: Inline .map() for dynamic list rendering in Astro templates
glossary.evidence: Lines 65-77 (architectural styles), 107-125 (included items) use .map() with JSX-style rendering inside Astro templates

glossary.name: Nav/Footer slot injection pattern in web-marketing
glossary.evidence: terms.astro passes <Nav slot='nav'> and <Footer slot='footer'> to BaseLayout — distinct from saltgrass-modular which uses no explicit slots

glossary.name: IntersectionObserver for sticky sidebar TOC active-state
glossary.evidence: Client-side script (lines 317-341) uses IntersectionObserver with rootMargin '-80px 0px -60% 0px' to highlight the TOC link matching the on-screen section

glossary.name: Mobile vs desktop TOC via accordion + sticky sidebar
glossary.evidence: Two parallel TOC implementations: mobile accordion (lines 41-58, JS toggle at 284-302) and desktop sticky sidebar (lines 65-79). Same sections array drives both.

glossary.name: Silent-fail / 204 empty response on backend error
glossary.evidence: s.ts, t.ts, and health.ts all catch errors silently and return either empty body or degraded status — explicit design choice to never surface backend errors to end users

glossary.name: Astro static site generation with LPAI backend as content source
glossary.evidence: blog/[slug].astro uses getStaticPaths() calling backend /api/blog/posts to enumerate slugs at build time — SSG pattern with LPAI as headless CMS

glossary.name: BaseLayout accepts keywords prop for SEO meta
glossary.evidence: developers.astro line 11 passes keywords prop to BaseLayout — extends the known BaseLayout prop surface beyond title/description

glossary.name: Modals scoped exclusively to index.astro DOM
glossary.evidence: Both quote-modal and consultation-modal divs are only rendered in index.astro; other pages reference them via onclick/data-action creating a cross-page dependency that silently fails on direct navigation

glossary.name: Hardcoded frontmatter data arrays for product and process content
glossary.evidence: container-homes.astro, pools.astro, and process.astro all define their primary content as static JS arrays in the Astro frontmatter — no LPAI backend fetch for product/pricing/stage data

glossary.name: Dead CTA button pattern on model catalog pages
glossary.evidence: 'Request Quote' buttons in container-homes.astro (line 157) and pools.astro (lines 104, 163) have no onclick, href, or form action — consistent unfinished wiring across catalog pages

glossary.name: Gradient CSS class string as image placeholder in data arrays
glossary.evidence: projects.astro stores Tailwind gradient strings in the image field consumed as bg- class suffix — signals placeholder state, not a real asset reference pattern

glossary.name: SSR redirect via Astro redirect() / new Response()
glossary.evidence: qr/[code].astro uses Astro's redirect() for 302 and `new Response('...', { status: 404 })` to terminate SSR — Astro SSR response-termination pattern

glossary.name: Gradient placeholder blocks instead of real images
glossary.evidence: container-homes.astro uses `bg-gradient-to-br` divs for model gallery and project gallery instead of tags — unfinished placeholder pattern that may ship to production

glossary.name: Three-import Astro service page pattern (BaseLayout + Hero + CtaBanner)
glossary.evidence: Both pools.astro and traditional-builds.astro import exactly these three components and no others — a repeating template for all saltgrass-modular service detail pages

glossary.name: Fully static service detail pages with hardcoded pricing
glossary.evidence: pools.astro and traditional-builds.astro contain no fetch/API calls; all product specs and pricing are hardcoded in the Astro template — any price change requires a code edit

glossary.name: Dead CTA buttons pattern on service pages
glossary.evidence: pools.astro lines 58/96 have <button> elements with no action; traditional-builds.astro CTA banner has no link target — consistent unfinished wiring across service pages

glossary.name: Nav/Footer slot injection pattern in web-marketing vs implicit in saltgrass-modular
glossary.evidence: terms.astro (web-marketing) uses explicit slot='nav' and slot='footer' on Nav/Footer components; saltgrass-modular pages use no slots — two different layout composition patterns across the two apps
